title: Classic Spaghetti Bolognese
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Finely chop the onion and garlic, then sauté until soft.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon.
Stir in the canned tomatoes and oregano. Simmer for 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the spaghetti in salted boiling water according to package instructions.
Drain the spaghetti and toss with the bolognese sauce. Serve immediately.

title: Grilled Salmon with Asparagus
instructions: Preheat the grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Pat the salmon fillets dry and season with salt and pepper.
Trim the woody ends off the asparagus and toss with olive oil and minced garlic.
Grill the salmon for 4–5 minutes per side until cooked through.
Grill the asparagus for 3–4 minutes, turning occasionally.
Serve the salmon and asparagus with lemon wedges on the side.

title: Chocolate Lava Cake
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C. Grease four ramekins with butter and dust with cocoa powder.
Melt the dark chocolate and butter together in a heatproof bowl over simmering water.
Whisk the eggs and sugar until pale and fluffy.
Fold the melted chocolate into the egg mixture, then sift in the flour and salt.
Stir in the vanilla extract until just combined.
Divide the batter between the ramekins and bake for 12–14 minutes.
The edges should be set but the center still soft. Invert onto plates and serve warm.

title: Strawberry Cheesecake
instructions: Crush the digestive biscuits into fine crumbs and mix with melted butter.
Press the crumb mixture firmly into the base of a 23 cm springform pan. Chill while preparing the filling.
Beat the cream cheese and sugar until smooth and creamy.
Add the heavy cream, vanilla extract, and lemon juice. Beat until thick and spreadable.
Spread the cheesecake filling over the chilled biscuit base and smooth the top.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight until set.
Top with fresh strawberries before serving.

title: Caesar Salad
instructions: Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper. Grill or pan-fry until cooked through, about 6–7 minutes per side. Let rest, then slice.
Wash and roughly chop the romaine lettuce into bite-sized pieces.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, Worcestershire sauce, and black pepper to make the dressing.
In a large salad bowl, toss the lettuce with the dressing until evenly coated.
Top with sliced chicken, croutons, and shaved parmesan cheese.
Serve immediately for the best crunch.
